message:
fix(core): insert rowid at INTEGER PRIMARY KEY position when loading B-tree data

Fix table loading from B-tree storage to correctly handle INTEGER PRIMARY KEY
columns which store their value as the rowid, not in the record payload.

## Problem
When a table has an INTEGER PRIMARY KEY column, SQLite stores that column's
value as the B-tree rowid rather than in the cell payload. The parse_record()
function returns values for all OTHER columns, but was missing the IPK column.

This caused:
- Wrong column alignment when reading rows with IPK
- Missing data in the IPK column position

## Solution
After parsing the record payload, check if the table has an INTEGER PRIMARY
KEY column (ipk_col_idx). If so, insert the rowid value at that position:

  if let Some(ipk_idx) = ipk_col_idx {
      values.insert(ipk_idx, SqliteValue::Integer(rowid));
  }

## Changes
- Extract ipk_col_idx outside the loop for efficiency
- Add explanatory comments about IPK column storage semantics
- Insert rowid at correct position after parse_record()

This ensures queries like "SELECT id, name FROM users" return correct
values when 'id' is INTEGER PRIMARY KEY.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
